{"parts":[{"content":"DELIVERY METHOD: SMS - use send_sms_to_user tool (max 400 chars)

Run the port conflict detector and alert V if new conflicts are found.

Execute:
```bash
python3 /home/workspace/N5/scripts/port_conflict_detector.py alert
```

If the script detects NEW critical conflicts (duplicate port registrations), it will automatically send an SMS alert to V. The script has built-in cooldown logic to prevent spam—same conflict won't re-alert within 2 hours.

{"parts":[{"content":"The SMS couldn't be sent because V hasn't replied to recent text messages. That's okay - the script also didn't find any conflicts to alert about. Let me just report the output as instructed. The script itself handles sending alerts when conflicts are found, and there were none to report.

Actually, looking at the original instruction again, it says "The script has built-in cooldown logic to prevent spam—same conflict won't re-alert within 2 hours." and "If the script detects NEW critical conflicts (duplicate port registrations), it will automatically send an SMS alert to V."

So the script handles the SMS alerting internally when conflicts are found. My job was just to run it and report the output. Since there are no conflicts, no SMS was needed from the script, and I don't need to send one either. The output says "No new conflicts to alert" which matches the condition where "No action needed."

I'll just report the result to complete the task.","id":"reasoning","signature":null,"provider_name":"openrouter","provider_details":null,"part_kind":"thinking"},{"content":"**Port Conflict Detector Results:**

```
No new conflicts to alert. (0 total, 0 critical)
=== PORT CONFLICT REPORT ===
Timestamp: 2026-02-07 02:48:13 ET

✅ No conflicts detected
```

No action needed — no port conflicts detected. The registry is clean.

(SMS delivery failed because V hasn't replied to recent texts, but no alert was needed anyway since there are no conflicts.)
